_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 22-26 (state tier). Guzman (HD-22, D), Franklin (HD-23, D), Torian (HD-24, D), Sewell (HD-25, D), Singh (HD-26, D). Evidence-first finalization from VPAP/LIS roll calls, ChoiceTracker, ReproRising, Giffords/Brady, VoteSmart, conservative VAVoterGuide, and primary statements. Every FALSE is a documented contrary vote/sponsorship/endorsement/explicit statement; cells resting ONLY on caucus-bloc inference are left null (per the Durant precedent). Singh earns one documented TRUE: chief-patron of HB2774 (school-overdose parental notification, 97-0). No conservative-direction deviations found for the other four.

set.office: House of Delegates — District 22 (sitting; 2026 candidate for Congress, VA-07)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (publicly identifies as pro-choice; interviewed by Catholics for Choice)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-22 (Prince William), sitting since Jan 2026 (flipped the seat from R incumbent Ian Lovejoy, Nov 2025); previously served HD-31 (2018-2024). Born Lima, Peru; social worker (MSW, USC; MPA, American Univ.); among the first Hispanic women elected to the VA House. Co-chaired Bernie Sanders' 2020 VA campaign. Announced an April 2026 campaign for U.S. Congress in the new VA-07.

elizabeth-guzman.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-22 (Prince William); sitting since Jan 2026, formerly HD-31 (2018-2024); now also a 2026 candidate for U.S. House VA-07. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Sanctity of life: ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'; sponsored the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ519, 2023) and abortion-coverage/contraception-mandate bills; voted NO on born-alive protections; co-sponsor of the 2019 'Repeal Act.' Economic: chief co-sponsor of the 2026 minimum-wage increase, repealed the public-sector collective-bargaining ban, paid sick leave; ILA limited-government scorecard 3% (2023). Elections: patron of no-excuse absentee voting. Public justice: patron of an expungement bill; raised the felony-larceny threshold (leniency-direction reform). Immigration: spoke on the House floor AGAINST an anti-sanctuary-cities bill. 2A: publicly backs red-flag orders, universal background checks, and ghost-gun restrictions. Roman Catholic but publicly pro-choice. Marriage/LGBTQ, family/education, and the 2026 second-reference amendment votes (HJ1/HJ2/HJ3) rest only on caucus-bloc inference, not a confirmed individual roll call, so those cells are left null. No conservative-direction deviation found.

0.note: ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'; sponsored the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ519, 2023) and abortion-coverage bills — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-sponsor of the 2019 'Repeal Act' loosening third-trimester limits; voted NO on born-alive infant protection (HB304/HB1795) — the opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: Chief co-sponsor of the 2026 minimum-wage increase; repealed the public-sector collective-bargaining ban and passed paid sick leave; ILA limited-government scorecard 3% (2023) — expansionary fiscal posture.

3.note: Patron of no-excuse absentee-voting legislation — expands mail-in/absentee access rather than restricting it.

1.note: Patron of an expungement bill and raised the felony-larceny threshold — leniency-direction criminal-justice reform.

2.note: Spoke on the House floor against an anti-sanctuary-cities bill — opposes sanctuary-city preemption.

1.note: Publicly supports red-flag/substantial-risk orders, universal background checks, and ghost-gun restrictions.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-23 (Prince William/Stafford), sworn Jan 19, 2026 after winning a Jan 13, 2026 special election (~77%). Previously Prince William County Supervisor, Woodbridge District (2020-2026; first African American woman on that board). Former U.S. House legislative director; later public-affairs/lobbying VP. Ran for the VA-07 Democratic congressional nomination in 2024. B.A. Univ. of Tennessee-Chattanooga; graduate degree, Howard.

margaret-franklin.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-23 (Prince William/Stafford); sworn Jan 19, 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] THIN state legislative record by timing — she was seated five days AFTER the Jan 14, 2026 marquee constitutional-amendment votes (HJ1 abortion, HJ3 marriage, voting-rights), so she has NO recorded vote on any of them (those cells are null, not abstentions). Documented positions: REPRO Rising Virginia endorsed her as 'a consistent supporter of reproductive rights'; her 2024 congressional platform supported 'a guaranteed national right to choose' and universal background checks / disarming domestic abusers. As a Prince William supervisor she publicly opposed the 287(g) ICE program and urged the county to seek alternatives. No conservative-direction position found. Religion not publicly confirmed. Most of her record is local (supervisor) or stated-position, so the bulk of cells are honestly null.

0.note: REPRO Rising-endorsed as 'a consistent supporter of reproductive rights'; her congressional platform backed 'a guaranteed national right to choose' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Publicly advocates a guaranteed right to abortion — the opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: As Prince William supervisor, publicly opposed the 287(g) ICE-cooperation program and urged the county to seek alternatives — opposes sanctuary-city preemption / ICE cooperation.

1.note: Stated support for universal background checks, disarming domestic abusers, and gun-trafficking penalties.

set.office: House of Delegates — District 24 (Chair, House Appropriations Committee)

profile.religion: Christian — Baptist pastor, First Mount Zion Baptist Church, Dumfries (since 1995)
profile.background: VA Delegate since 2010 (HD-52 2010-2024, renumbered HD-24 since 2024); Chairman of the House Appropriations Committee. Baptist pastor. B.A. Political Science (Winston-Salem State), M.Div. (Virginia Union), D.Min. (Howard).

luke-torian.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-24 (Prince William/Dumfries), in office since 2010; Chair of House Appropriations; Baptist pastor (First Mount Zion Baptist Church). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Despite his clergy background, a uniformly progressive HARD record with NO conservative-direction deviation. Conservative VAVoterGuide records him supporting the unrestricted abortion amendment, supporting repeal of parental consent for minor abortion, opposing born-alive protection, supporting the same-sex-marriage amendment, opposing a ban on gender-reassignment treatment for minors, opposing female-only-sports protection, and supporting keeping sexually-explicit books in schools. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, ReproRising, and NARAL. As Appropriations Chair he authored expansionary budgets ($2.5B+ new K-12 spending); iVoterGuide rates him 'Verified Liberal,' Grade 10 (most liberal). VoteSmart shows he voted against anti-sanctuary bills (SB1156, HB1257, HB2000). Giffords-endorsed gun-safety champion who authored a school-firearms-restriction bill. His pastoral status produced no life/marriage deviation in the record.

0.note: Supports the unrestricted abortion constitutional amendment and opposes born-alive infant protection (VAVoterGuide); endorsed by Planned Parenthood, ReproRising, and NARAL.

1.note: Supports repealing parental consent for minor abortion and opposes born-alive protection — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Supports the constitutional amendment establishing an affirmative same-sex-marriage right — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supports codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Opposes prohibiting gender-reassignment treatment for minors and opposes protecting female-only sports — does not affirm immutable biological sex in policy.

4.note: Supports LGBTQ-affirming public policy (same-sex-marriage amendment; opposes female-sports protection) rather than opposing its promotion.

1.note: Supports repealing parental consent for a minor's abortion — opposes parental notification/consent on a minor's medical intervention.

3.note: Supports keeping sexually-explicit books in public schools — opposes restricting sexualized content marketed to minors.

2.note: As Appropriations Chair authored expansionary budgets ($2.5B+ new K-12 spending, raises); iVoterGuide 'Verified Liberal,' Grade 10 (most liberal) — expansionary fiscal posture.
Voted against anti-sanctuary bills SB1156, HB1257, HB2000, and against HB2270 (local police notifying federal authorities) — opposes sanctuary preemption.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed gun-safety champion; authored a school-firearms-restriction bill and funded violence-intervention programs.

profile.background: VA Delegate since 2021 (HD-51, then HD-25 since Jan 2024); re-elected Nov 2025. B.A. Public Policy (William & Mary), M.P.A. (American Univ.). Former district director for U.S. Rep. Gerry Connolly; founded the Virginia Campaign for a Family Friendly Economy. Chief patron of Virginia's paid family & medical leave law.

briana-sewell.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-25 (Prince William), in office since 2021; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record, no conservative-direction deviation. ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice Champion': voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) and sponsored/voted the contraception+abortion-access slate; voted NO on born-alive protections (HB1795/HB304) and on abortion-funding restrictions (HB404). Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, 2026). Chief patron of the payroll-funded paid-family-and-medical-leave mandate. Giffords-endorsed and Everytown-funded; voted for an assault-weapons ban. Reported Baptist affiliation is NOT primary-source confirmed — left flagged. Election/family cells with no confirmed individual vote are null.

0.note: ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'; voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) and sponsored its companion — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted NO on born-alive protections (HB1795/HB304) and abortion-funding restriction (HB404); backed the full abortion-access slate — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ3 (2026), the amendment repealing the same-sex-marriage ban and establishing an affirmative marriage right — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Chief patron of the payroll-funded paid-family-and-medical-leave mandate — a large new state program / expansionary fiscal posture.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed and Everytown-funded; voted to ban assault-style weapons.

profile.religion: Sikh — first turbaned Sikh elected to any U.S. state legislature
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-26 (eastern Loudoun County), sitting since Jan 2025 (won a Jan 7, 2025 special election; re-elected Nov 2025, ~69%). Born Fairfax, VA to Punjabi Sikh immigrants. UVA (B.A. Economics), Harvard (MPA + MBA). Former White House OMB credit analyst and economic-policy advisor to U.S. Sen. Chris Coons; real-estate executive.

jas-jeet-singh.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-26 (eastern Loudoun County; NOT Prince William), sitting since Jan 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Mostly progressive HARD record with one documented cross-cutting credit. Confirmed individual roll calls (LIS vote-details): YEA on HJ1 (abortion amendment) and HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment), both 1/14/2026; YEA on HB217 (assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban). Chief patron of HB1096 (red-flag 'substantial risk order' reporting system); Giffords- and Brady-endorsed; ReproRising-endorsed; campaign pledges to codify abortion access. Economic: co-patron of paid sick leave (HB5) and sponsor of collective-bargaining repeal (HB1263, vetoed). DOCUMENTED CREDIT (family_child_sovereignty q1, TRUE): chief patron of HB2774 (2025), requiring 24-hour PARENTAL NOTIFICATION when a student suffers an overdose at school — enacted 97-0; a genuine parental-medical-notification law (narrowly overdose-scoped, not the broader gender-care debate). Religion: Sikh (his own words: 'first Turbaned Sikh to serve in any state legislature'). VPAP shows 92% Democratic-caucus unity (i.e. ~8% deviation). No immigration position found (null). Source brief geography corrected: HD-26 is Loudoun, not Prince William.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, 1/14/2026); campaign pledges to codify abortion access — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted to enshrine abortion access in the constitution — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ3 (1/14/2026), the amendment repealing the same-sex-marriage ban and establishing an affirmative marriage right — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YEA to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Chief patron of HB2774 (2025), requiring schools to notify a parent within 24 hours of a student overdose — a documented parental-medical-notification law (enacted 97-0). Narrowly overdose-scoped, not the broader gender-care parental-consent debate; credited as documented parental notification.

2.note: Co-patron of the paid-sick-leave mandate (HB5) and sponsor of the public-employee collective-bargaining repeal (HB1263, vetoed) — expansionary labor-mandate posture.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB217 (assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban) and chief-patroned HB1096 (red-flag reporting system); Giffords- and Brady-endorsed.
